rules.name: console-output
rules.message: Console output — use a structured logger (spdlog, quill) instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with spdlog::info/debug/warn/error or equivalent structured logger.

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager; load at runtime.

rules.name: using-namespace-std
rules.message: using namespace std — pollutes the global namespace and causes ambiguity.
rules.fix_hint: Use std:: prefix or selective using declarations (using std::string;).

rules.name: raw-new-delete
rules.message: Raw new/delete — use smart pointers (std::unique_ptr, std::make_unique) instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with std::make_unique<T>() or std::make_shared<T>() for RAII ownership.

rules.name: catch-ellipsis
rules.message: Catch-all (...) — catches all exceptions including system errors.

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns.

rules.name: magic-numbers
rules.message: Magic number in comparison — extract to a named constant for clarity.
rules.fix_hint: Define a constexpr or const variable with a descriptive name.

rules.name: volatile-for-sync
rules.message: volatile for synchronization — it does not provide atomicity or memory ordering.
rules.fix_hint: Use std::atomic<T> for thread-safe shared state.

rules.name: empty-catch
rules.message: Empty catch block silently swallows exceptions.
rules.fix_hint: Log the error, rethrow, or add a comment explaining why it is intentionally ignored.
